Romano Rancho  ·  Southern Portugal

A farm. A forest. A way of life.

A small, family-run farm
growing within a living
cork oak forest.

Where the trees are older than memory.

Who we are

A family living and growing here, together.

Romano Rancho is a small, family-run farm set within a living cork oak forest in southern Portugal. We are raising our children among ancient, protected cork oak trees — some of them hundreds of years old.

These trees are never cut down. Their bark is harvested only once every nine years, allowing the forest to remain alive, productive, and gloriously diverse. The cork oak is patient. So are we.

This farm was created as a way of life — not as a tourist attraction. We chose a slower, low-impact path: reducing our carbon footprint, sourcing locally, avoiding waste, and building only what is truly needed.

The family behind the farm

We are Romano
Rancho.

Two parents, two children, one enormous cork oak forest in southern Portugal. We didn't come here to open a business — we came here to live differently. Slowly. Honestly. Closer to the land.

When guests arrive, they don't check into a hotel. They step into our world — our mornings, our trees, our homemade food. We think that makes all the difference.

9
Years between
each bark harvest
100s
Years old —
the cork oaks here
1
Family. One forest.
One story.
How we choose to live here

Every decision guided
by one principle:
leave the land better.

🌿
Low Impact

We reduce our carbon footprint at every step — sourcing locally, avoiding waste, and building only what is truly necessary.

🔕
Slow Living

Limited signal. Few distractions. Plenty of quiet. Visitors are invited to slow down and explore the forest at their own pace.

🌳
The Trees First

The cork oaks are never cut. Everything grown, built, and offered here is designed around the health and longevity of the forest.

🤝
Real Exchange

Every meal, every handmade item, every moment spent here helps us plant, grow, and care for this land. A real and honest exchange.

"Romano Rancho is not a place to consume. It is a place to pause, connect, and become part of a growing story."

Come and disconnect

Visitors who come here are invited to explore the cork oak forest freely — no agenda, no schedule, no performance.

Everything supports the land

Food, handmade items, time spent here — each small choice directly supports the care of the trees and the continued life of this place.

A living story

We are not finished. This farm is always growing, planting, improving. When you visit, you become part of what it is becoming.

Before you arrive

Not a campsite.

Not a row of vans. Not a number on a list.

Not this
  • Rows of caravans side by side
  • Noise from strangers all night
  • A reception desk and a wristband
  • Shared facilities with 80 people
  • A pitch number and checkout time
  • Staff who don't know your name
This
  • 54,000 m² of living cork oak forest — nearly all yours
  • One small family. One land. Real quiet.
  • A homemade breakfast cooked that morning
  • Children playing freely, animals nearby
  • A hammock between two trees older than your grandparents
  • People who will remember you when you leave

We are not a business pretending to be nature.
We are a family who lives here — and sometimes, we invite guests in.

🌱

Ancient trees.
A family growing alongside them.

We are glad you found us.
Come slowly. Stay quietly. Leave gently.

From our kitchen

Honest food,
eaten outdoors.

Every morning we cook fresh. Eggs from our hens, homemade hummus, granola made with care. The kind of breakfast worth waking up for.

Join the forest

Adopt a Cork Oak

Slow down. The forest is older than all of us.

Some things grow quietly, over time.

By adopting a cork oak, you're choosing to care for a living forest and to be part of a place that grows slowly and thoughtfully. Your support helps us plant, tend the land, and protect the ancient cork oaks that shape Romano Rancho.

From time to time, we'll share gentle updates — a photo, a season changing, a small moment from the forest — so you can feel connected, even from afar.

No ownership. No expectations. Just the quiet joy of knowing you helped something living grow.

You're always welcome back.
